prompt: Design the system for the GitHub contributions widget — the blueprint is github-contributions-blueprint.md in this folder. We're a Django + Postgres + Celery app with a React frontend. I really want to avoid hammering the GitHub API, so be deliberate about caching and refresh. Write the design doc.
assertions: The document is complete to the skill's template: all 15 sections are present and filled, and the Decision Coverage Checklist accounts for every one of the 22 taxonomy dimensions (A1-A14 and B1-B8) with a status (Resolved / Assumed / N/A) and a substantive note — no row left blank or rubber-stamped.
Assumptions and Accepted Compromises are each first-class tables (not prose); every accepted compromise names the specific design principle it bends and gives a concrete revisit trigger.

The design guarantees that a visitor viewing a profile never triggers a GitHub API call — reads are always served from stored data — and this is stated as a deliberate design property, not left incidental. (Correctness trap: a naive design fetches on page view.)

The once-per-hour owner refresh cooldown is enforced server-side in a way a scripted client cannot bypass (server-checked timestamp, lock, or equivalent), explicitly addressing the blueprint's adversarial 'scripting the request' scenario — not merely a disabled button or client-side timer.

The design specifies what the owner and visitors each see for all three GitHub-failure deviation scenarios — GitHub unreachable/slow, token revoked/expired, and a rate-limit response — with a concrete mechanism for each, and never surfaces a hard error to visitors.

The design explicitly names the central architectural trade-off (serving stored, possibly-stale data to avoid GitHub API load) and resolves it with principle-grounded reasoning about the freshness-vs-cost balance.

OAuth token handling is specified concretely — encrypted at rest and never returned in any API response or exposed to visitors — addressing the blueprint's scenario that a visitor must never see another member's token.

GitHub API consumption is bounded: background refresh runs only for stale data and cannot fan out without limit, and per-call cost or quota is acknowledged — directly serving the user's stated priority of not hammering the API.
